title: dNTP (Deoxyribonucleotide Triphosphate)
summary: A free nucleotide carrying three phosphate groups (tri-phosphate). When DNA polymerase adds it to a growing strand, it cleaves off the two outer phosphates, releasing pyrophosphate; this provides the energy for the new phosphodiester bond. The four DNA dNTPs are dATP, dTTP, dGTP, dCTP. PCR requires dNTPs as the raw material for new DNA synthesis.
